outline.headline: Oracle HCM 26B Integration Options
outline.message: Options analysis for the IT steering committee on how to integrate Oracle HCM 26B with our existing HR systems.

outline.headline: What We'll Cover
outline.message: Context, three integration options, comparison matrix, recommendation, and next steps.

outline.headline: 26B Introduces 14 New REST Endpoints
outline.message: Oracle HCM 26B significantly expands API coverage for position management, reducing the need for custom extracts.
evidence_queries: Oracle HCM 26B new REST API endpoints for position management
Comparison of 26B API coverage versus 25D for HR data access
Position management endpoints deprecation timeline in 26B release notes

outline.headline: Positions vs Jobs Model Requires Migration
outline.message: The 26B positions-vs-jobs restructuring changes how org hierarchy data flows, requiring a one-time migration before go-live.
evidence_queries: Oracle HCM positions versus jobs data model changes in 26B
Org hierarchy migration steps required for positions-vs-jobs transition
Impact of positions-vs-jobs change on downstream integrations

outline.headline: Three Integration Options
outline.message: We evaluated full API, HCM Extract, and a hybrid approach against cost, risk, and timeline criteria.

outline.headline: Option A: Full API — Lowest Cost, Highest Effort
outline.message: Real-time data via REST APIs. Lowest ongoing cost but requires 12-week build for custom position sync.
evidence_queries: Full API integration approach implementation timeline and effort estimate
Ongoing maintenance cost estimate for full API approach to Oracle HCM
Technical risks of full API approach including rate limiting and error handling

outline.headline: Option B: HCM Extract — Fastest, but 24h Lag
outline.message: Batch file extracts deployed in 4 weeks but introduce up to 24-hour data freshness lag on position changes.
evidence_queries: HCM Extract approach deployment timeline and configuration effort
Data freshness lag characteristics of HCM Extract for position data
Limitations of HCM Extract approach for real-time reporting needs

outline.headline: Option C: Hybrid — Best Balance of Speed and Fidelity
outline.message: Real-time position data via API plus batch payroll via extract. Moderate effort, best long-term flexibility.
evidence_queries: Hybrid integration approach combining API and HCM Extract for Oracle HCM
Implementation timeline and cost estimate for hybrid approach
Long-term maintainability advantages of hybrid integration pattern

outline.headline: Hybrid Wins on 4 of 5 Evaluation Criteria
outline.message: Side-by-side comparison shows hybrid option scores highest on data fidelity, flexibility, ongoing cost, and risk, trailing only on initial timeline.
evidence_queries: Evaluation criteria scores for all three Oracle HCM integration options
Data fidelity comparison across full API, HCM Extract, and hybrid approaches
Total cost of ownership comparison for three integration options over 3 years

outline.headline: Recommend Hybrid — Approve by April 30
outline.message: Proceed with hybrid approach, begin org migration in May, target go-live for August 26B release window.
evidence_queries: Recommended implementation timeline milestones for hybrid approach
Dependencies and prerequisites for August go-live with Oracle HCM 26B
